title: Rising Inflation and Global Tensions Impact U.S. Spending Habits
description: U.S. consumer spending increased in January amid higher prices and ongoing international conflicts, suggesting that the Federal Reserve is unlikely to cut interest rates soon. Although spending rose, adjusted figures for inflation show minimal growth. Global tensions and energy price spikes are straining consumer confidence and economic outlook.

title: US stock market Dow Jones, S&P 500, Nasdaq crash: Why Dow, S&P 500 and Nasdaq are down today? Nvidia, Nebius and Oracle stocks move
description: US stock market Dow Jones, S&P 500, Nasdaq crash: The Dow Jones fell 390 points to 47,316. The S&P 500 slipped to 6,765. The Nasdaq held near 22,687. Investors reacted to fresh US CPI inflation data, which rose 0.3% monthly and 2.4% yearly. Oil prices also jumped near $86 a barrel amid global supply worries. These pressures weighed on the Dow Jones and S&P 500. However, tech stocks moved higher. Nvidia backed AI cloud firm Nebius with a $2 billion investment. Oracle stock surged after strong earnings and $17.2B revenue.

title: Why is Dow Jones surging today? Dow jumps nearly 400 points - Will S&P 500 and Nasdaq extend the US stock market rally?
description: Dow Jones today: The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose to 48,133, up about 0.82%, while the S&P 500 climbed to 6,834 and the Nasdaq Composite reached 22,865. Investors returned to equities after oil prices dropped near $84 per barrel, easing inflation fears. Tech and semiconductor stocks like NVIDIA and Intel helped lift the market. At the same time, traders balanced risks from Middle East tensions and Iran conflict headlines. Wall Street is now watching upcoming CPI inflation data and major tech earnings. If inflation cools, analysts expect the Dow, S&P 500, and Nasdaq rally to extend.

description: Deutsche Bank has upgraded technology sectors in the US and Europe. Software is now an overweight. Fears of AI disruption have likely ended. Valuations are now thin.
